questions.question: Express the following as a single sine, cosine or tangent: sin 15° cos 20° + cos 15° sin 20°
questions.explanation: Use the identity sin(A + B) = sin A cos B + cos A sin B. Here A = 15° and B = 20°, so sin(15° + 20°) = sin 35°.

questions.question: Express the following as a single sine, cosine or tangent: cos 130° cos 80° – sin 130° sin 80°
questions.explanation: Use the identity cos(A + B) = cos A cos B – sin A sin B. Here A = 130° and B = 80°, so cos(130° + 80°) = cos 210°.

questions.question: Use the formulae for sin(A – B) and cos(A – B) to show that tan(A – B) = (tan A – tan B) / (1 + tan A tan B)
questions.explanation: Substitute addition expansions for sine and cosine, then divide every term by (cos A cos B) to convert to tangents.
options: Multiply numerator and denominator by sin A sin B
Divide numerator and denominator by sin A cos B
LHS = sin(A-B)/cos(A-B) = (sin A cos B - cos A sin B) / (cos A cos B + sin A sin B). Dividing numerator and denominator by cos A cos B gives the result.
Substitute sin(A−B) = sin A + sin B − 2cos A cos B

question_types.title: Using the angle addition formulae 7B — Exact Trigonometric Values

questions.question: Without using your calculator, find the exact value of cos 15°
Without using your calculator, find the exact value of sin 75°

question_types.title: Using the angle addition formulae 7B — Evaluating Multi-part Expressions

questions.question: Given that sin A = –3/5 and 180° < A < 270°, and that cos B = –12/13 and B is obtuse, find the value of cos(A – B)
questions.explanation: In 3rd quadrant, cos A = –4/5. Since B is obtuse (2nd quadrant), sin B = 5/13. cos(A – B) = cos A cos B + sin A sin B = (–4/5)(–12/13) + (–3/5)(5/13) = 48/65 – 15/65 = 33/65.

question_types.title: Double-angle formulae 7C — Simplifying to Single Ratios

questions.question: Write the expression as a single trigonometric ratio: 2 sin 10° cos 10°
questions.explanation: Using sin 2A = 2 sin A cos A with A = 10°, we get sin(2 × 10°) = sin 20°.

questions.question: Write the expression as a single trigonometric ratio: cos² 40° – sin² 40°
questions.explanation: Using cos 2A = cos² A – sin² A with A = 40°, we get cos(2 × 40°) = cos 80°.

questions.question: Without using your calculator, find the exact value of 2 sin 22.5° cos 22.5°
questions.explanation: This is in the form 2 sin A cos A = sin 2A. sin(2 × 22.5°) = sin 45° = 1/√2 (or √2/2).

questions.question: Without using your calculator, find the exact value of 2 cos² 15° – 1
questions.explanation: This is in the form 2 cos² A – 1 = cos 2A. cos(2 × 15°) = cos 30° = √3/2.
